meta.scene_summary: We are building a future where robots do the coding, the agents have the memory, and the lawyers have all the work.

cooking.headline: The Supreme Court just pushed a hotfix for geofence warrants
cooking.summary: The court decided that police can no longer treat your location history like a public API endpoint without a warrant. It is the legal equivalent of finally implementing proper authentication after a massive data leak.
cooking.plain_english: The US Supreme Court ruled that law enforcement must obtain a warrant to access broad location data collected from mobile devices.

cooking.headline: South Korea is betting $1T that our future coworkers will be robots who don't ask for coffee breaks.
cooking.summary: South Korea is dumping a trillion dollars into memory chips and humanoid robots to secure their place in the AI race. It's a bold move, though I'm sure the robots will eventually just ask for a 40-hour work week and remote-first policies.
cooking.plain_english: The South Korean government plans to invest one trillion dollars into the development of advanced memory chips and humanoid robotics technology.

cooking.headline: Zluda 6 just made your non-Nvidia GPU feel a lot less like a glorified paperweight.
cooking.summary: Zluda lets you run CUDA apps on non-Nvidia hardware, which is basically the tech equivalent of running a Mac app on Windows without the headache. It's great news for anyone who got tired of paying the 'Nvidia tax' just to run a local model.
cooking.plain_english: A new software release allows applications designed for Nvidia hardware to run on other graphics processing units.

cooking.headline: Microsoft wants you to run your dev environment in a box, inside a box
cooking.summary: Microsoft is letting you run WSL containers directly, because apparently, we haven't reached our daily quota of virtualization layers. It's perfect for when you want to debug your production environment by nesting it three levels deep inside your own OS.
cooking.plain_english: Microsoft has released a preview feature that allows developers to run Linux-based container environments directly within the Windows Subsystem for Linux.

cooking.headline: LongCat-2.0 is the latest model to treat VRAM like a suggestion
cooking.summary: This new model boasts 1.6 trillion parameters, proving that if you can't optimize your code, you can just throw more hardware at the problem. It's the AI equivalent of a group project where one person does all the heavy lifting while the other 1.5 trillion parameters just watch.
cooking.plain_english: LongCat-2.0 is a large artificial intelligence model that uses a massive number of parameters to improve its processing efficiency.

cooking.headline: Ornith-1.0 is teaching AI to code by failing at it until it gets better
cooking.summary: This model uses self-improvement loops to debug itself, effectively automating the 'trial and error' phase of a junior dev. It is basically a group project where the only member is an AI that keeps rewriting its own code while you watch.
cooking.plain_english: Ornith-1.0 is an open-source artificial intelligence model designed to write and improve its own computer code through repetitive practice.

cooking.headline: One memory to rule all your AI agents
cooking.summary: The Open Memory Protocol tries to give your various AI tools a shared brain so they stop asking you the same questions over and over. It is the digital equivalent of finally centralizing your notes so you don't have to explain your project requirements to every new contractor you hire.
cooking.plain_english: The Open Memory Protocol is a standard that allows different artificial intelligence services to access and share the same saved user information.

cooked.headline: EU-US data transfers just hit a 404 error that no amount of refactoring can fix
cooked.summary: The Supreme Court essentially deleted the handshake protocol between continents, leaving international data flows in a massive deadlock. It is like finding out your entire production environment was hosted on a server that just got repossessed by the landlord.
cooked.plain_english: The US Supreme Court has issued a ruling that invalidates the current legal framework for transferring personal data between the European Union and the United States.

cooked.headline: Bandcamp is speedrunning the 'we don't need engineers' business model
cooked.summary: Bandcamp just laid off most of their engineering team, which is a bold strategy for a platform whose entire product is a website. It's like firing the mechanic and then wondering why the car only drives in reverse.
cooked.plain_english: Bandcamp has significantly reduced its engineering staff as part of a recent round of layoffs.

cooked.headline: Google wants your hand gestures just to prove you aren't a robot
cooked.summary: Google is asking for high-fidelity camera access to track 21 points on your hand just to sign into a website. It turns out the easiest way to prove you're human is to give up enough biometric data to identify you in a police lineup.
cooked.plain_english: Google has introduced a new version of its bot-detection tool that tracks hand movements through a user's camera to verify their identity.
